text.title: Go Standard Layout Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed when applying go-standard-layout. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix keyed to the methodology.

antipattern.symptom: Handler function is 200 lines including validation, query orchestration, and conditional rendering.
antipattern.root-cause: Author kept adding 'one more thing' to handler instead of pushing to service.
antipattern.fix: Extract logic into service. Handler reduces to decode + service.X(ctx, primitives) + encode.

antipattern.symptom: Service file imports net/http to read a header from a request struct.
antipattern.root-cause: Author passed the *http.Request to service instead of extracting primitives at the handler.
antipattern.fix: Extract values at handler. Service takes primitives. Add depguard rule blocking net/http import in service packages.

antipattern.symptom: Handler calls db.QueryContext directly to short-circuit a repo call.
antipattern.root-cause: Author wanted 'just a quick query' without a repo method.
antipattern.fix: Add a repo method. Handler calls service which calls repo. Block database/sql import in handler packages.

antipattern.symptom: Repo declares an interface that exposes 23 methods; each service uses 3 of them.
antipattern.root-cause: Author wrote interface at repo to expose 'API surface'.
antipattern.fix: Move interfaces to consumer side. Each service declares the 3 methods it needs. Repo exports a concrete struct.
